title: serratula boetica subsp. legionensis
intro: Serratula boetica subsp. legionensis is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of burnet-saxifrage or Legion burnet. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Serratula boetica subsp. legionensis include its ability to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
